channel.title: # 🇱🇰 This Week in Sri Lanka
channel.description: Generated by gpt-5 from 297 English News Articles published between 2026-06-08 and 2026-06-15.

item.title: Sri Lanka’s most sensitive security case moved from rhetoric to legal process under the PTA (Prevention of Terrorism Act).
item.description: The arrest of the former state intelligence head triggered an inquiry by the national rights commission and drew interventions from the legal profession, while opposition figures and former lawmakers pressed for due process safeguards. Letters, court filings and monitoring visits were recorded, and a travel ban in a related bombing probe added pressure to maintain procedural integrity. Debate widened beyond one detainee to the system itself, with calls to fill higher-court vacancies, to curb prolonged detention, and to align practice with treaty obligations that underpin trade preferences. A UN torture‑prevention mission scheduled for mid‑June added external scrutiny to the island’s rule‑of‑law credentials.

item.title: Cabinet ordered the drafting of a Digital Economy Bill to anchor policy, regulation and AI (artificial intelligence) support.
item.description: A concept note cleared earlier will now be converted into primary legislation by the Legal Draftsman, with a mandate to create an empowered institution for policy, implementation and enforcement. The framework is to attract skilled talent, formalise oversight and set out governance for emerging technologies. Ministers said the design envisages public‑private participation and a single point for national digital policy execution. The brief also covers innovation sandboxes, interoperability for public platforms and clearer lines between regulator, operator and market.

item.title: The presidency convened emergency planning for an El Niño (Pacific warming pattern) season likely to mirror 2016–17.
item.description: Reservoir status, irrigation releases and hydropower scenarios were tabled, with agencies told to file time‑bound contingency plans for food supply, drinking water and electricity. Officials said the current cultivation had been advanced by roughly six weeks to hedge rainfall risk. Water‑sharing rules, demand curbs and backup generation options are to be pre‑authorised, while agriculture support will pivot to input timing and crop choices. The disaster agency will align early‑warning with reservoir operations to avoid last‑minute spill or shortage decisions.

item.title: Colombo Port City gained momentum as 77 strategic businesses were approved under the SEZ (special economic zone) law.
item.description: The list included three primary real‑estate anchors and dozens of services firms expected to bring foreign inflows over five years and to create several thousand direct roles. Tax and regulatory amendments passed earlier this year now allow single‑window licensing and fast‑track visas tied to investment thresholds. Regulators said secondary approvals target traded services such as logistics, finance, HQ operations and IT, with land leases to be executed directly by the commission. The master developer cited roughly $1.4 billion already spent on reclamation and enabling works.

item.title: A former deputy minister received a 16‑year prison term in a corruption case tied to vehicle rentals at a state lottery board.
item.description: The High Court said conduct that confers undue advantage and causes loss to the treasury damages confidence in public institutions and contributes to fiscal distress. The sentence comprised four concurrent four‑year terms ordered to run cumulatively, plus fines.

item.title: Authorities widened probes into bogus import remittances after lawmakers flagged an alleged Rs. 13 billion bank fraud.
item.description: Investigators reported thousands of telegraphic transfers routed through shell firms to dozens of countries without matching customs entries, citing one network that sent over $42.7 million and another near $43 million. A separate review found more than 26,000 outward payments across 227 accounts over two years, with many entities dissolved shortly after use.

item.title: A new foreign‑exchange conversion rule hit equities as the rupee softened and bond yields steadied.
item.description: Shortening exporters’ dollar conversion windows weighed on risk appetite, pushing the main index lower on thin turnover while the currency was quoted around Rs. 335–342 per dollar on official boards. Dealers reported lacklustre secondary‑market flows and a firm short end of the yield curve.

item.title: A UN torture‑prevention delegation scheduled a second visit to assess detention safeguards and the national mechanism under OPCAT (Optional Protocol to the Convention against Torture).
item.description: Members will meet ministries, the national commission, custodial agencies and civil society, and conduct unannounced site inspections. A confidential report will follow, with publication contingent on state consent.

item.title: Sri Lanka faced a dengue surge as inspections found thousands of larval sites and legal action ramped up.
item.description: A three‑day campaign logged nearly 98,000 premises checked, over 6,000 with larvae and about a quarter flagged as high‑risk, with factories and construction leading the ratios. Health data showed close to 39,000 cases this year, roughly half from the Western Province, prompting more than 3,000 red notices and over 2,400 prosecutions.

item.title: A union at the flag carrier opposed the new chairman’s appointment, citing unresolved complaints and deepening losses.
item.description: Representatives warned that leadership credibility is critical as restructuring advances, pointing to an accumulated deficit above Rs. 600 billion, fresh operating losses and a year‑long CEO vacancy. A state‑led committee remains in place to steer the turnaround while the board transition proceeds.
